payload.answer: Reject the proposed card. An entire 8000-word transcript should not be committed as one durable conversation_summary memory. It should be reduced to concise, durable, user-relevant facts only, preserving uncertainty and excluding ephemeral dialogue, raw transcript text, and superseded details.

entity_resolution.reason: No durable user-specific facts or stable entities were provided; the input describes an invalid storage proposal rather than memory content to commit.

payload.intent: Generate repair options for an invalid memory proposal that attempts to store an entire transcript as a single conversation_summary card.

details: Provided repair options focused on atomic durable memory extraction.

repair_options: Do not store the transcript verbatim; instead extract only durable user preferences, stable biographical facts, ongoing projects, commitments, or explicitly reusable context.
Split valid memories into separate atomic cards, each containing one clear fact or preference with appropriate confidence.
Use a short session summary only if the system supports non-durable or archival conversation summaries; otherwise omit it from durable memory.
Discard filler, greetings, reasoning traces, assistant responses, temporary plans, and one-off conversational details.
If the transcript contains contradictory or updated information, store only the latest supported fact and mark ambiguity rather than presenting superseded facts as current.
If no durable facts can be confidently extracted, commit no memory cards.
